ksas.statement: Ability to distinguish between problems that require machine learning and problems that can be solved more simply and reliably with rule-based logic or statistical methods.
Knowledge of supervised and unsupervised machine learning algorithms, including their mathematical foundations, hyperparameter behavior, and appropriate application conditions.
Knowledge of statistical inference methods, including hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, Bayesian inference, and the assumptions underlying each method.
Knowledge of model evaluation frameworks, including train/validation/test split design, cross-validation, evaluation metric selection, and the conditions under which each metric is appropriate.

Knowledge of feature engineering techniques, including encoding strategies, normalization, handling of missing data, feature selection methods, and the relationship between feature quality and model performance.
Knowledge of model interpretability and explainability methods, including SHAP values, LIME, partial dependence plots, and when explanation requirements should influence model selection.
Skill in formulating business problems as machine learning problems, including defining the prediction target, identifying appropriate inputs, and establishing measurable success criteria.
Skill in conducting exploratory data analysis, including distributional analysis, correlation analysis, anomaly detection, and translating EDA findings into modeling decisions.
Skill in writing production-quality Python code for data processing, feature engineering, model training, and evaluation, including documentation and unit testing.
Skill in communicating model findings and limitations to non-technical business stakeholders in terms of business impact rather than technical metrics.
Conduct exploratory data analysis on new datasets, documenting distributional characteristics, data quality findings, and implications for modeling approach.
Design, train, evaluate, and document machine learning models, including feature engineering decisions, algorithm selection rationale, and evaluation results against baseline.
Prepare model documentation for production deployment, including model card, performance characteristics, known limitations, and monitoring recommendations.
Conduct post-deployment model monitoring, identifying performance degradation, data drift, and conditions requiring model retraining or replacement.
Knowledge of ML model serving patterns, including REST API serving, batch inference, streaming inference, edge deployment, and the latency, throughput, and cost trade-offs of each.
Knowledge of containerization and orchestration technologies (Docker, Kubernetes) as applied to ML model packaging, deployment, and scaling.
Knowledge of ML pipeline frameworks and the engineering practices required to make training and inference pipelines reproducible, testable, and maintainable.
Knowledge of model monitoring approaches, including prediction drift detection, input data distribution monitoring, and latency and throughput tracking.
Skill in refactoring research-quality data science code into production-grade, tested, documented, and deployable ML systems.
Skill in designing and implementing model serving APIs, including input validation, output formatting, error handling, authentication, and versioning.
Skill in implementing automated ML retraining pipelines triggered by performance degradation signals or data distribution shifts.
Package and deploy ML models to production serving infrastructure, including containerization, API wrapping, load testing, and rollback procedures.
Implement model monitoring dashboards and alerting, including performance degradation thresholds, drift detection, and incident response procedures.
Build and maintain ML training pipelines, including data ingestion, preprocessing, training, evaluation, and model registration steps.
Knowledge of large language model capabilities, limitations, failure modes, and the prompt engineering and system design patterns that shape reliable LLM-based application behavior.
Knowledge of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) architecture patterns, including vector store design, chunking strategies, retrieval evaluation, and hallucination mitigation approaches.
Knowledge of agentic AI design patterns, including tool use, multi-agent coordination, memory systems, and the safety and reliability challenges specific to agentic architectures.
Knowledge of AI application security risks, including prompt injection, jailbreaking, data exfiltration via LLM outputs, and model supply chain vulnerabilities.
Skill in designing and evaluating prompt engineering strategies, including few-shot prompting, chain-of-thought prompting, and structured output generation.
Skill in building and evaluating RAG pipelines, including embedding model selection, retrieval quality measurement, and end-to-end system evaluation.
Skill in implementing AI application guardrails, including input validation, output filtering, safety classifiers, and human-in-the-loop intervention points.
Design and build LLM-based application systems, including system prompt design, tool integration, retrieval pipeline, output validation, and failure mode documentation.
Evaluate AI application performance against defined quality, safety, and reliability criteria, documenting test results and identified failure modes.
Implement AI application security controls, including prompt injection defenses, output filtering, access controls, and audit logging.

Knowledge of NLP task taxonomy, including classification, named entity recognition, relation extraction, summarization, translation, and question answering, and the model architectures appropriate for each.
Knowledge of transformer architecture, attention mechanisms, and the pre-training and fine-tuning paradigm as it applies to language models.
Knowledge of NLP evaluation frameworks, including BLEU, ROUGE, BERTScore, and human evaluation methodologies and their appropriate application contexts.
Skill in fine-tuning pre-trained language models for domain-specific tasks, including dataset preparation, training configuration, and evaluation design.
Skill in designing and implementing NLP evaluation pipelines that measure task-specific performance, robustness, and fairness.
Design and implement NLP systems for defined business tasks, including model selection, data preparation, fine-tuning, evaluation, and deployment documentation.
Conduct linguistic analysis and error analysis on NLP system outputs, documenting failure patterns and recommending model or data improvements.
Knowledge of computer vision task taxonomy, including image classification, object detection, segmentation, pose estimation, and generation, and the architectures appropriate for each.
Knowledge of CNN architectures, vision transformer architectures, and the data augmentation, regularization, and transfer learning techniques applicable to visual recognition tasks.
Skill in preparing image and video datasets for model training, including annotation pipeline design, quality control, augmentation strategy, and train/validation split construction.
Skill in training, evaluating, and optimizing computer vision models, including performance profiling, inference optimization, and edge deployment considerations.
Design and build computer vision systems for defined business applications, including dataset preparation, model training, evaluation, deployment, and performance monitoring.
